title: Non-Invertible Symmetries and Boundaries for Two-Dimensional Fermions
authors: Guillermo Arias-Tamargo, Philip Boyle Smith, Rishi Mouland, Maxwell L. Velásquez Cotini Hutt
abstract: We study the relation between boundary conditions and categorical symmetries of two-dimensional fermionic conformal field theories. We determine all anomaly-free invertible global symmetries of two free complex Weyl fermions, which take the form $\mathbb{Z}_k$ for each primitive Pythagorean triple $a^2 + b^2 = k^2$. The theory is self-dual under gauging any of these symmetries, and so to each there is associated a non-invertible topological defect. We study the properties of these lines, and show that any conformal boundary condition of two Dirac fermions that preserves a $U(1)^2$ symmetry can be found by dressing a trivial Dirichlet boundary with one of them. We discuss two microscopic descriptions of these defects: fermions coupled to a quantum-mechanical rotor degree of freedom; and an abelian gauge theory that realises symmetric mass generation in a half-space.
summary: This paper classifies the non-invertible symmetries and boundary conditions for 2D fermionic systems. It establishes a deep connection between number theory (Pythagorean triples) and the existence of topological defects and symmetric boundaries in conformal field theories.
main_problem: The paper investigates the relationship between boundary conditions and non-invertible (categorical) symmetries in 2D fermionic Conformal Field Theories (CFTs). Specifically, it seeks to classify anomaly-free invertible symmetries and their associated non-invertible topological defects.
main_result: The authors identify all anomaly-free invertible symmetries of two free complex Weyl fermions as cyclic groups Z_k related to primitive Pythagorean triples, and show that the theory is self-dual under gauging these symmetries. They further demonstrate that all U(1)^2 symmetric conformal boundary conditions for two Dirac fermions can be constructed by dressing a Dirichlet boundary with these non-invertible lines.
method: The study employs modular transformation analysis of partition functions, symmetry gauging, and number-theoretic classification using Gaussian integers and Pythagorean triples. Microscopic realizations are provided via fermion-rotor coupling and Abelian gauge theories implementing symmetric mass generation.
model_or_system: The primary system is a 2D Conformal Field Theory consisting of two free complex Weyl fermions. The study also considers two Dirac fermions on a manifold with a boundary (half-line).
key_observables: Partition functions in different spin structures (NS-NS, R-NS, NS-R, R-R), central charge, and scaling dimensions of operators.
important_parameters: The integer k = p^2 + q^2 (from coprime integers p and q), the gauging parameter t, and the mass parameters for the fermions.
main_assumptions: The symmetries considered are assumed to be faithful, discrete, and invertible 0-form symmetries.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the relationship between boundary conditions and non-invertible lines; Figure 2 shows modular S and T transformations for different spin structures; Figure 11 and 14 depict interfaces and boundary conditions involving Majorana modes.
paper_structure: The paper begins by classifying anomaly-free invertible symmetries, moves to demonstrating the self-duality of the theory under gauging these symmetries, then constructs non-invertible defects and uses them to define conformal boundary conditions, and concludes with microscopic UV realizations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
